title: Obscurity-Is-Dead — AI-Assisted Hacking: Key to Interoperability or Security Nightmare?
description: Research-as-code repository for the paper 'AI-Assisted Hacking: Key to Interoperability or Security Nightmare?'. Documents two empirical case studies (Spider Farmer BLE; EcoFlow PowerOcean) plus a recursive third case study of the paper-generation process itself. Treats AI conversation transcripts, vendor artifacts, and version history as first-class evidence. Explicitly addresses sloppification of science and model collapse. Adheres to DFG Guidelines for Safeguarding Good Research Practice and to the FAIR data principles (see docs/fair.md).
notes: License (CC-BY-4.0) confirmed by the author and recorded in the top-level LICENSE file. The licence covers the human-authored and human-curated portions of the work; vendor artifacts vendored under experiments/*/original/ carry their own redistribution caveats per docs/sources.md. Statement of independence: this is a hobbyist project carried out by the author in a personal capacity. It is not part of, endorsed by, funded by, or representative of the views of any employer, including DLR (Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt). See paper/main.md \S 9.5 and the README disclaimer. AI authorship: the AI assistant (Claude, Anthropic) is acknowledged as a contributor in \S 9.1 but is not a co-author. Under \S 2 UrhG (German Copyright Act), copyright protection requires a 'persönliche geistige Schöpfung' (personal intellectual creation) by a human; AI outputs do not on their own qualify. See the footnote on Urheberrecht und KI in paper/main.md \S 9.1 / paper/main.tex sec:ai-disclosure for sources.
